evidence_summary: Using character level information can improve vector representations for morphologically rich languages.
Representing words as the sum of character n-gram vectors can be beneficial.
Replacing the embedding layer with a parametrized neural network that processes characters of a given word can allow the model to consume arbitrary words and is not restricted to a fixed vocabulary.
